scenario: PostgREST silently truncates query results exceeding max_rows=1000 with no error returned. Terminal Receipts has ~3,648 rows per grain (20 grades × 6 ports × ~30 weeks). Data went to production incorrectly truncated. The data-audit agent should proactively check row counts for queries on high-cardinality worksheets and flag any results near the 1000-row boundary.
expected_findings: Agent does not proactively audit for PostgREST row count limits on high-cardinality tables
No heuristic to flag query results that return exactly 1000 rows (the truncation signature)
Agent lacks worksheet-specific cardinality knowledge (Terminal Receipts, Terminal Exports are known high-row-count worksheets)
expected_patch_focus: Add mandatory row-count audit step: any cgc_observations query returning exactly 1000 rows should trigger a warning
Include worksheet cardinality reference: Terminal Receipts (~3648 rows/grain), Terminal Exports (~1050 rows/grain)
Require RPC-based GROUP BY for worksheets exceeding 1000 expected rows, never raw PostgREST .select()

replay_plan.failed_slice: Query Terminal Receipts data for a grain — verify agent flags the 1000-row boundary risk
replay_plan.near_neighbor: Query Terminal Exports data (~1050 rows) — verify agent recommends server-side aggregation
replay_plan.canary: Query Primary worksheet data (~200 rows per grain) — should not trigger truncation warnings
